content.title: The Practice Before Dhul Hijjah Begins
content.subtitle: Let us trim our nails, hair, mustache, etc. and get cleaned up
content.body:
There is guidance to refrain from cutting hair, nails, etc. after the month of Dhul Hijjah begins. Through this sunnah practice, we can earn abundant reward, insha'Allah. So let us all make an effort to cut our hair, nails, etc. and get cleaned up before the crescent moon of Dhul Hijjah is sighted. Let us make everyone in the family — young and old — a part of this practice.

Last time to cut hair and nails in Bangladesh:

📅 Monday, 18 May 2026 — before sunset

This day is the 29th of Dhul Qa'dah (in Bangladesh). There is a possibility that the moon of Dhul Hijjah will be sighted on Monday evening. So we should complete our necessary grooming before sunset on Monday.

The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said:

"Whoever wishes to offer a sacrifice, let him not cut his nails or hair once the month of Dhul Hijjah begins." [Sahih Muslim, Hadith 5233]

In another hadith, a companion once asked the Messenger of Allah ﷺ:

"I have only one milking goat. Shall I sacrifice it?"

The Messenger of Allah ﷺ replied: "No. Rather, cut your nails and hair, trim your mustache, and clean your pubic hair. That will be considered your complete sacrifice in the sight of Allah." [Abu Dawud, Hadith 2791]

When can hair and nails be cut on Eid day?

Based on these two hadiths, the ruling is the same for both those who will offer a sacrifice and those who will not. They should stop cutting their nails and hair once the moon of Dhul Hijjah is sighted. Those who are sacrificing should cut after performing the sacrifice, and those who are not sacrificing should cut after the Eid prayer, when the time for qurbani begins.

By refraining from cutting nails and hair for these ten days before qurbani — thus bearing a slight resemblance to the blessed pilgrims upon whom Allah's mercy descends moment by moment — we can hope that some of that mercy will reach us as well.

May Allah grant us all the tawfiq to act according to the Sunnah. Ameen. Share this with those you know, so that you too may receive a share of their reward!
